subject: Chemistry
title: Hennig Brand Discovers Phosphorus
description: Learn Hennig Brand Discovers Phosphorus in one minute.

hook.text: In 1669, a German alchemist boiled 1,500 gallons of urine and accidentally discovered a new element.

buildup.text: Hennig Brand was searching for the philosopher's stone. He thought gold might hide in human urine.

discovery.text: After weeks of boiling and heating, a waxy substance glowed in the dark. He had found phosphorus.

twist.text: Brand kept it secret to profit from it. But others soon repeated his method and published it.

climax.text: Phosphorus was the first element discovered by a known person. It opened the age of element hunting.

punchline.text: The first new element came from boiled urine. Science starts weird.

quiz.question: What was Hennig Brand originally trying to find when he discovered phosphorus?
